commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: CBOT SRW wheat at $6.18/bushel (April 1). Up from pre-war $5.62. Nearing 2-year peak. Fertilizer disruptions from Hormuz closure (+40% costs) driving 2026 crop increases. UN humanitarian/fertilizer corridor through Hormuz agreed March 27. GCC food imports disrupted 70%.
commodities.source: USDA Minneapolis Daily Grain Report / CME March 30

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: CBOT May corn at $4.56/bushel (April 1). Up from pre-war $4.31. USDA Prospective Plantings showed corn acreage down 3.45M acres YoY as fertilizer costs shift acres to soybeans. USDA projects $4.20 marketing year average.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: CBOT soybeans (May) at $11.61/bushel. Up 14.7% from pre-war $10.12. Managed money at 4-year high net long in grains. Soybean oil surging on biofuel demand. Palm oil also elevated. One-third of globally traded fertilizer transits through Hormuz.
commodities.source: Brownfield Ag News / CME March 20 closing

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Sugar #11 (ICE) at $0.142/lb — actually DOWN from pre-war $0.21. Global sugar market has a 3.4 million metric ton surplus forecast for 2026/27; the Iran war has not materially elevated sugar prices. Unlike energy-linked grains, sugar supply/demand dynamics dominated by global harvest conditions.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Palm oil: shipping insurance costs up 10x (0.25% to ~10% of vessel value per Lloyd's List), spiking transport costs. War risk insurance adds $3-5/MT to global commodity delivery.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged from $475 to $695/tonne. Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. Worst timing: start of Northern Hemisphere planting season. Wolfe Research estimates food-at-home inflation could rise ~2 percentage points. USDA predicts 3.1% food price rise in 2026.

context: War-driven supply disruptions continue to push food prices higher. Fertilizer costs up 40% (urea $700+/tonne, ammonia +92% YoY in ME, +41% in US). ~1/3 of global fertilizer transits Hormuz. USDA predicts 3.1% food price rise in 2026. WFP: 45M more could face acute hunger. Diesel at $5.45/gal rippling through food transport. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA Prospective Plantings: corn acreage down 3.45M acres YoY. GCC food imports disrupted 70%. Gas $4.06/gal adds to transportation costs.
